source.license_notes: Invented mock entries — not a real dictionary
source.limitations: Does not establish regulatory, legal, or technical truth

records.title: authority
records.text: Authority: the recognized right to direct, decide, or enforce within a defined scope.

definitions.authority: recognized right within a defined scope

claims: Authority is always scoped to a domain of competence

records.title: claim
records.text: Claim: a statement presented for evaluation; not truth until validated within scope.
claims: A claim is not proof by itself

records.title: scope
records.text: Scope: the boundary within which a definition, rule, or standard applies.
claims: A source can be authoritative within one scope and irrelevant outside it

records.title: standard
records.text: Standard: a documented agreement on practice, often versioned and domain-specific.

records.title: jurisdiction
records.text: Jurisdiction: the geographic or legal territory where a rule or regulation applies.

records.title: evidence
records.text: Evidence: material offered to support reasoning; must be evaluated, not assumed true.

records.title: memory
records.text: Memory: retained state from prior events; retention requires governance policy.
claims: A passed scan is not permission for memory

records.title: identity
records.text: Identity: exact sameness; distinct from similarity or resemblance.

records.title: similarity
records.text: Similarity: resemblance between entities without implying identical identity.
